name: NVIDIA Corporation
headline: The default pick-and-shovel trade on the entire AI buildout.

children.text: NVIDIA's data center business has gone from a quiet niche to the most important revenue stream in semiconductors in roughly thirty months. Hyperscalers, sovereigns, and enterprises are all competing for the same constrained supply of Blackwell-class GPUs, and pricing power has held even as competitors push their own accelerators.

The bull case isn't 'AI is real' — that's already in the price. The bull case is that the next phase (inference at scale, sovereign AI factories, robotics) is bigger than training, and NVIDIA's CUDA moat plus networking stack (Mellanox, Spectrum-X) keep it the default platform for years.

bullCase: Generational lead in AI training and inference; CUDA software moat is a multi-year switching cost.
Networking (Mellanox / Spectrum-X / NVLink) makes NVIDIA's stack the path of least resistance at hyperscale.
Sovereign AI buildouts and enterprise inference are still in the early innings.
Operating margins above 60% give massive room to absorb price pressure.
Optionality across robotics, autonomous, and Omniverse industrial digital twins.
bearCase: Customer concentration: a handful of hyperscalers drive most of the data center revenue.
ASIC competition (TPU, MTIA, Trainium) chipping at the edges, especially in inference.
Geopolitical risk on China revenue and TSMC dependency.
At current valuation, any pause in capex from the top three customers gets priced in violently.
High inventory + high prepayments mean a 1-quarter air pocket if demand normalizes.

name: Microsoft Corporation
headline: Cloud, productivity, and a front-row seat to the AI commercialization wave.

children.text: Microsoft converted its OpenAI relationship into measurable Azure revenue faster than the bears thought possible. The Copilot attach rate inside the Microsoft 365 estate is the slower-burning prize — billions of seats already paying, where every dollar of ARPU lift is almost pure margin.

What sets it apart from the rest of the megacap cohort is the combination of distribution (already in every enterprise) and balance sheet (capex without strain). The risk isn't the AI cycle, it's whether the multiple can hold while capex peaks.

bullCase: Azure AI is now a meaningful and accelerating contributor to cloud growth.
Copilot attach across M365 is a slow-burn ARPU expansion that compounds for years.
Enterprise distribution moat is unmatched — every CIO is already a customer.
Capital allocation is best in class: dividends growing, buybacks consistent, balance sheet pristine.
Gaming + LinkedIn provide diversification most software peers can't match.
bearCase: AI capex is enormous and depreciation will hit operating margins for several years.
OpenAI relationship has become noisier; commercial terms could be renegotiated.
Multiple expansion has done a lot of the recent work — fundamentals must keep up.
Regulatory scrutiny on Activision integration and AI partnerships is rising.
Slowing on-prem and traditional Windows OEM still drag at the edges.

name: Apple Inc.
headline: A hardware empire quietly turning into a high-margin services compounder.

children.text: The iPhone unit story has been flattish for years and that's fine — Services is now a $90B+ business growing double digits at 70% gross margins. The installed base of 2.2 billion active devices is the asset, and Apple has barely begun monetizing it the way the market's already pricing.

Apple Intelligence has been a slow rollout, but the strategic point is that AI features become a reason to upgrade, and a reason to stay locked into the ecosystem. The risk is more boring: regulators, China, and a multiple that already reflects compounder status.

bullCase: Services revenue is now the second-biggest line and growing 12-15% with software-like margins.
Installed base of 2.2B+ devices is an unmatched distribution asset for any new product or service.
Apple Silicon transition unlocks margin and unique product capabilities.
Capital return program returns ~$100B/year to shareholders.
Optionality on Vision Pro and AI services if either accelerates.
bearCase: iPhone unit growth is structurally slow; Services has to do all the lifting.
China is both a massive market and a structural risk — competitive pressure is intensifying.
DOJ and EU regulatory pressure on App Store economics is real and growing.
Apple Intelligence has been a slow, mixed rollout vs. the marketing.
Premium multiple gives little room for error if Services growth decelerates.

name: Shopify Inc.
headline: Canada's flagship tech name and the operating system for independent commerce.

children.text: Shopify made one of the cleanest pivots in recent tech history when it sold the logistics business and refocused on the high-margin merchant solutions and subscription stack. Free cash flow generation is now real and growing, and operating leverage on the existing GMV base is finally showing up.

The bull thesis is that Shopify keeps moving up-market (Plus, enterprise) and gets a higher take rate from each merchant via Shop Pay, Capital, and B2B. The bear thesis is that growth has matured and the multiple is still pricing in hyper-growth.

bullCase: Operating margins re-expanding now that the logistics distraction is gone.
Shop Pay and Shopify Capital extracting more take rate per merchant.
Enterprise traction with the Shopify Plus and B2B offerings is real.
Strong global brand and developer ecosystem keeps the merchant flywheel turning.
Founder-led with long-term capital allocation discipline.
bearCase: Macro-sensitive — GMV swings with consumer discretionary spend.
Competition from Amazon Multi-Channel, Stripe, and Adobe Commerce is real at the high end.
Margin re-expansion may already be in the price.
Currency headwinds for Canadian-reporting investors when USD weakens.
Heavy stock-based comp continues to dilute shareholders.

catalysts.label: Black Friday / Cyber Monday GMV print

name: Royal Bank of Canada
headline: Canada's largest bank and a dividend-growth cornerstone for domestic portfolios.

children.text: RBC is the closest thing the TSX has to a defensive compounder. The HSBC Canada acquisition expanded the domestic moat, capital markets is high quality, and wealth management keeps doing the heavy lifting on growth. The dividend has been raised through every recent cycle.

bullCase: Canada's largest bank by assets and capitalization with diversified revenue across personal, commercial, capital markets, and wealth.
HSBC Canada acquisition adds high-net-worth deposits and meaningful synergy potential.
Wealth management business is one of the best in the country.
Strong capital position and multi-decade track record of dividend increases.
US City National banking franchise is now stabilized after writedowns.
bearCase: Canadian housing market exposure is meaningful — any sharp downturn flows through to provisions.
Net interest margin sensitivity to where the BoC takes rates.
HSBC integration risk and elevated severance/restructuring costs in the near term.
Currency translation hits when reporting US revenue back to CAD.
Slower aggregate growth than US peers — this is income, not a growth story.

name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
headline: The best-run megabank in America, and the benchmark every other lender is measured against.

children.text: JPMorgan has emerged from the rate cycle with a fortress balance sheet, a leading deposit franchise, and a capital markets business that takes share whenever the cycle turns its way. The Dimon premium is real but not unwarranted — there's a reason regulators kept calling it the rescuer of last resort.

bullCase: Diversified across investment banking, consumer, commercial, and asset & wealth management.
Industry-leading return on tangible equity and best-in-class operating efficiency.
Excess capital position gives flexibility for buybacks and counter-cyclical loan growth.
Trading and IB benefit from market volatility and the eventual re-acceleration of M&A.
Largest US deposit franchise — a structural funding advantage.
bearCase: Net interest income is past peak as the rate cycle normalizes.
Credit normalization will lift loan loss provisions and pressure earnings.
Regulatory capital requirements continue to creep higher post-Basel III endgame.
Jamie Dimon succession is a multi-year overhang.
Premium valuation versus peers leaves less room for execution missteps.

name: Eli Lilly and Company
headline: The GLP-1 franchise that turned a 150-year-old pharma into a growth stock.

children.text: Mounjaro and Zepbound have rewritten the addressable market for cardiometabolic disease, and Lilly's manufacturing capacity expansion is finally starting to catch up with demand. The pipeline behind GLP-1 (oral orforglipron, retatrutide, Donanemab in Alzheimer's) gives multiple shots on goal beyond the obesity story.

bullCase: GLP-1 demand still constrained by manufacturing — capacity expansion unlocks revenue runway.
Pipeline (orforglipron oral, retatrutide triple agonist, donanemab) gives multiple acts beyond GLP-1.
Pricing power as long as supply is constrained and outcomes data keeps improving.
Long patent runway on tirzepatide vs. peers nearing patent cliffs.
Operating leverage on capacity additions is significant once they come online.
bearCase: Compounding pharmacies and competitor pipelines (Novo, Pfizer oral, Roche, Amgen) erode pricing later this decade.
Manufacturing miss or supply shock could swing a quarter sharply.
Pricing pressure from PBMs and government payors is rising.
Valuation prices in near-perfect execution.
Cardiovascular outcomes / safety surveillance always carries tail risk.

name: Enbridge Inc.
headline: North America's pipeline backbone — and one of the most reliable dividend payers on the TSX.

children.text: Enbridge is closer to a regulated utility than a commodity producer. Cash flow comes from long-term contracted volumes on the Mainline, Gas Transmission, and a growing US gas utilities footprint after the Dominion acquisitions. The dividend has been raised for 29 consecutive years.

bullCase: Long-life infrastructure with predictable, contracted cash flows.
US gas utilities acquisition adds regulated rate-base growth.
Renewables segment provides optionality on the energy transition.
Dividend yield among the highest in any large-cap North American name.
Heavily inelastic demand — pipelines stay full through commodity cycles.
bearCase: High debt load is a known overhang and constrains capital allocation flexibility.
Permitting and pipeline construction risk in both Canada and the US.
Regulatory and Indigenous consultation processes continue to slow large new builds.
Energy transition narrative pressures terminal value of crude pipeline volumes.
Currency translation drags reported earnings when CAD strengthens.

name: Canadian Natural Resources Limited
headline: Long-life, low-decline reserves and a management team obsessed with returning cash to shareholders.

children.text: CNQ has the lowest break-even of any large-cap Canadian producer and management explicitly tells you what they'll do with every dollar of free cash flow above their net debt target. That kind of clarity is rare in commodity-exposed names.

bullCase: Low decline, long-life reserves require minimal sustaining capex.
Free cash flow allocation framework is one of the clearest in the sector.
Variable dividend and buybacks return excess capital efficiently.
Diversified across heavy oil, light oil, oil sands, and natural gas.
Strong record of operational execution through commodity cycles.
bearCase: Oil price volatility — earnings track WTI and WCS differentials.
Federal emissions cap policy creates regulatory uncertainty for oil sands operators.
ESG-driven capital outflows from the sector continue at the margin.
Heavy crude differentials can blow out and dent realized prices.
Currency translation when CAD strengthens against USD.

name: Costco Wholesale Corporation
headline: A membership-fee flywheel disguised as a retailer — and one of the most beloved compounders in the market.

children.text: Costco's earnings model is mostly the membership fee, with merchandise margins kept deliberately thin to keep the value proposition irresistible. Renewal rates above 90% globally and Executive member growth give Costco arguably the best customer cohort data in retail.

bullCase: Membership renewal rates above 90% globally — best in retail.
Pricing power on annual fee increases without renewal-rate damage.
International store expansion runway is still meaningful.
E-commerce and Costco Logistics building a credible online flywheel.
Recession-resistant trade-down dynamics keep traffic high in tough macro.
bearCase: Premium valuation reflects compounder status — limited margin for error.
Inflation and SKU mix can pressure thin merchandise margins.
Wage and benefit costs continue to climb (and Costco famously won't squeeze workers).
Capital intensity to maintain warehouse network is non-trivial.
Mature US market means growth depends increasingly on fees and international stores.
